title: Rich Polish Meat Stew
description:
Funky, flaky, cozy polish dish featuring shredded meat, dashi-extract, ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly.
Serve in an earthenware bowl for a rustic touch.
instructions: Heat butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown lamb shanks on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove from pot.
Lower heat to medium and add salt, pepper, and a pinch of caraway seeds to pot, stirring to combine.
Add a dashi extract and simmer for 1 minute, scraping up browned bits from bottom of pot.
Add a handful of fresh thyme sprigs and stir to combine.
Return lamb shanks to pot and cover with a lid, transferring to a 300°F oven for 2 hours.
Remove pot from oven and let cool slightly before shredding meat with two forks.
Add shredded meat back to pot and stir to combine with juices and thyme sprigs.
Simmer stew over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed before serving hot in an earthenware bowl.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Serve with warm rye bread or over egg noodles, if preferred.
